Thoroughly analyze all worker implementations in /Users/anotonio.silva/antonio/mms/system/src/workers/ and compare against spec requirements.

Expected workers per spec 002/004:
1. ingestion_worker - CSV parsing, validation, DB insertion
2. optimizer_worker - C++ binary orchestration, stream I/O
3. tactical_scheduler - 12-week PM scheduling
4. strategic_planner - 1-10 year genetic algorithm planning
5. manhour_leveling_worker - Weekly/monthly workload balancing
6. drift_monitor_worker - Nightly baseline comparison
7. report_worker - PDF/Excel generation
8. notification_worker - Email alerts
9. maximo_sync - Background Maximo synchronization (optional)

For each worker check:
- Is the processor fully implemented or has placeholders?
- Does it have proper error handling and retry logic?
- Does it emit proper metrics and traces?
- Are there any TODO comments?

Also check:
- Worker runner/daemon entry point
- BullMQ queue configuration
- Concurrency settings per worker type

Return detailed findings on implementation completeness.

   163‚Üí### 2.3.1 Daily Operational Flow
   164‚Üí
   165‚Üí```
   166‚Üí06:00 ‚îÇ CSV file drop (mileage.csv) ‚Üí File Watcher detects
   167‚Üí06:05 ‚îÇ Ingestion Worker: Parse, validate, insert mileage readings
   168‚Üí06:10 ‚îÇ Ingestion Worker: Update train current_mileage, detect anomalies
   169‚Üí06:15 ‚îÇ Drift Monitor: Check against baseline, flag significant deviations
   170‚Üí06:30 ‚îÇ Optimizer Worker: Generate today's run assignments
   171‚Üí      ‚îÇ   - Input: trains, routes, constraints, inventory positions
   172‚Üí      ‚îÇ   - Output: assignments with explanation traces
   173‚Üí07:00 ‚îÇ WebSocket: Push results to connected canvas clients
   174‚Üí07:00 ‚îÇ Operators: Review assignments on Infinite Canvas
   175‚Üí      ‚îÇ   - View explanation factors for each assignment
   176‚Üí      ‚îÇ   - Drag-and-drop to override if needed (with audit trail)
   177‚Üí08:00 ‚îÇ Export: Approved assignments sent to OCC system
   178‚Üí16:00 ‚îÇ Actual mileage updates (if available intra-day)
   179‚Üí22:00 ‚îÇ Optimizer Worker: Generate tomorrow's assignments

   182‚Üí### 2.3.2 Weekly Planning Flow
   183‚Üí
   184‚Üí```
   185‚ÜíMonday 06:00  ‚îÇ Strategic Planner: Run 10-year horizon update
   186‚Üí              ‚îÇ   - Uses constraint envelope from tactical model
   187‚Üí              ‚îÇ   - Respects published baseline (drift threshold)
   188‚ÜíMonday 06:30  ‚îÇ Tactical Scheduler: Run 12-week PM scheduling
   189‚Üí              ‚îÇ   - Check material availability for T+14 days (hard)
   190‚Üí              ‚îÇ   - Check material availability for T+90 days (soft)
   191‚ÜíMonday 07:00  ‚îÇ Man-hour Leveling: Generate 12-week projection
   192‚Üí              ‚îÇ   - Identify overloaded/underutilized weeks
   193‚Üí              ‚îÇ   - Propose adjustments (defer/advance/overtime)
   194‚ÜíMonday 08:00  ‚îÇ WebSocket: Push updated canvas data to all clients
   195‚ÜíTuesday       ‚îÇ Planners: Review PM conflicts on canvas
   196‚Üí              ‚îÇ   - Drag to resolve conflicts (audit trail)
   197‚Üí              ‚îÇ   - Review/approve man-hour adjustments
   198‚ÜíWednesday    ‚îÇ Report Worker: Generate resource utilization report
   199‚ÜíFriday       ‚îÇ Notification Worker: Send weekly compliance report
   200‚Üí```

   202‚Üí### 2.3.3 Baseline Publishing Flow
   203‚Üí
   204‚Üí```
   205‚ÜíUser Action  ‚îÇ Planner clicks "Publish Baseline" on canvas
   206‚Üí             ‚îÇ   - System validates: no critical conflicts
   207‚Üí             ‚îÇ   - System prompts: "This will create Baseline v3.2"
   208‚ÜíAPI Call     ‚îÇ POST /api/v1/baselines
   209‚Üí             ‚îÇ   - Snapshot current pm_schedule to baseline_pm_schedule
   210‚Üí             ‚îÇ   - Snapshot mileage projections to baseline_mileage_projection
   211‚Üí             ‚îÇ   - Set previous baseline status to SUPERSEDED
   212‚Üí             ‚îÇ   - Set new baseline status to PUBLISHED
   213‚ÜíPost-Publish ‚îÇ Drift Monitor: Now compares against new baseline
   214‚Üí             ‚îÇ Canvas: Shows drift layer relative to new baseline
   215‚Üí```
   216‚Üí
   217‚Üí### 2.3.4 Scenario Comparison Flow
   218‚Üí
   219‚Üí```
   220‚ÜíUser Action  ‚îÇ Planner clicks "Create Scenario" on canvas
   221‚Üí             ‚îÇ   - System forks from current published baseline
   222‚Üí             ‚îÇ   - New scenario_id assigned
   223‚ÜíEditing      ‚îÇ Planner makes changes within scenario
   224‚Üí             ‚îÇ   - All changes stored as deltas (event-sourced)
   225‚Üí             ‚îÇ   - Original baseline data unchanged
   226‚ÜíComparison   ‚îÇ Planner clicks "Compare Scenarios"
   227‚Üí             ‚îÇ   - System materializes both scenarios
   228‚Üí             ‚îÇ   - Canvas shows side-by-side diff view
   229‚Üí             ‚îÇ   - Metrics: cost impact, resource utilization delta
   230‚ÜíPromotion    ‚îÇ Planner clicks "Promote to Baseline"
   231‚Üí             ‚îÇ   - Deltas applied to create new baseline
   232‚Üí             ‚îÇ   - Scenario marked as PROMOTED
   233‚Üí```

   387‚Üí
   388‚Üí// Metrics (to stderr, OpenTelemetry format)
   389‚Üíinterface MetricsMessage {

   391‚Üí  timestamp: number;
   392‚Üí  metrics: {

   394‚Üí    'optimizer.branches_explored': number;

   406‚Üí
   407‚Üí// CRITICAL: Error handling messages (addresses silent failure risk)
   408‚Üí// See Gap Analysis GAP-003 for rationale
   409‚Üíinterface ErrorMessage {
   410‚Üí  type: 'ERROR';
   411‚Üí  request_id: string;
   412‚Üí  error_code: 'SEGFAULT' | 'TIMEOUT' | 'OOM' | 'INVALID_INPUT' | 'INTERNAL_ERROR';
   413‚Üí  error_message: string;
   414‚Üí  stack_trace?: string;  // When available
   415‚Üí  last_progress?: {
   416‚Üí    percent: number;
   417‚Üí    branches_explored: number;
   418‚Üí    memory_mb: number;
   419‚Üí  };
   420‚Üí  recovery_suggestion: string;  // Human-readable
   421‚Üí}
   422‚Üí
   423‚Üí// Heartbeat messages (sent every 5 seconds during solve)
   424‚Üíinterface HeartbeatMessage {
   425‚Üí  type: 'HEARTBEAT';
   426‚Üí  request_id: string;
   427‚Üí  timestamp: number;
   428‚Üí  memory_mb: number;
   429‚Üí  thread_count: number;
   430‚Üí  branches_per_second: number;
   431‚Üí}
   432‚Üí```
   433‚Üí
   434‚Üí### 2.4.4 Error Handling Protocol
   435‚Üí
   436‚Üí**CRITICAL:** The C++ optimizer communicates via stdin/stdout streams. Stream failures (segfaults, hangs) must be detectable by the TypeScript worker.
   437‚Üí
   438‚Üí```typescript
   439‚Üí// Worker-side error handling (TypeScript)
   440‚Üíclass OptimizerWorker {
   441‚Üí  private process: ChildProcess;
   442‚Üí  private heartbeatTimeout: NodeJS.Timeout | null = null;
   443‚Üí  private lastHeartbeat: number = 0;
   444‚Üí  
   445‚Üí  private readonly HEARTBEAT_INTERVAL_MS = 5000;
   446‚Üí  private readonly HEARTBEAT_TIMEOUT_MS = 15000;  // 3 missed heartbeats
   447‚Üí  
   448‚Üí  async solve(request: OptimizerInput): Promise<OptimizerOutput> {
   449‚Üí    return new Promise((resolve, reject) => {
   450‚Üí      // Start heartbeat monitor
   451‚Üí      this.startHeartbeatMonitor(request.request_id, reject);
   452‚Üí      
   453‚Üí      // Handle stream events
   454‚Üí      this.process.stdout.on('data', (chunk) => {
   455‚Üí        for (const line of chunk.toString().split('\n')) {
   456‚Üí          if (!line.trim()) continue;
   457‚Üí          
   458‚Üí          try {
   459‚Üí            const msg = JSON.parse(line);
   460‚Üí            
   461‚Üí            switch (msg.type) {
   462‚Üí              case 'HEARTBEAT':
   463‚Üí                this.lastHeartbeat = Date.now();
   464‚Üí                break;
   465‚Üí                
   466‚Üí              case 'PROGRESS':
   467‚Üí                this.emitProgress(msg);
   468‚Üí                break;
   469‚Üí                
   470‚Üí              case 'RESULT':
   471‚Üí                this.stopHeartbeatMonitor();
   472‚Üí                resolve(msg);
   473‚Üí                break;
   474‚Üí                
   475‚Üí              case 'ERROR':
   476‚Üí                this.stopHeartbeatMonitor();
   477‚Üí                reject(new OptimizerError(msg));
   478‚Üí                break;
   479‚Üí            }
   480‚Üí          } catch (parseError) {
   481‚Üí            this.logger.warn({ line }, 'Failed to parse optimizer output');
   482‚Üí          }
   483‚Üí        }
   484‚Üí      });
   485‚Üí      
   486‚Üí      // Handle process crash
   487‚Üí      this.process.on('exit', (code, signal) => {
   488‚Üí        if (code !== 0) {
   489‚Üí          this.stopHeartbeatMonitor();
   490‚Üí          reject(new OptimizerError({
   491‚Üí            type: 'ERROR',
   492‚Üí            request_id: request.request_id,
   493‚Üí            error_code: signal === 'SIGSEGV' ? 'SEGFAULT' : 'INTERNAL_ERROR',
   494‚Üí            error_message: `Process exited with code ${code}, signal ${signal}`,
   495‚Üí            recovery_suggestion: 'Check optimizer logs and input data'
   496‚Üí          }));
   497‚Üí        }
   498‚Üí      });
   499‚Üí      
   500‚Üí      // Send request
   501‚Üí      this.process.stdin.write(JSON.stringify(request) + '\n');
   502‚Üí    });
   503‚Üí  }
   504‚Üí  
   505‚Üí  private startHeartbeatMonitor(requestId: string, reject: Function): void {
   506‚Üí    this.lastHeartbeat = Date.now();
   507‚Üí    
   508‚Üí    this.heartbeatTimeout = setInterval(() => {
   509‚Üí      const elapsed = Date.now() - this.lastHeartbeat;
   510‚Üí      
   511‚Üí      if (elapsed > this.HEARTBEAT_TIMEOUT_MS) {
   512‚Üí        this.stopHeartbeatMonitor();
   513‚Üí        
   514‚Üí        // Kill hung process
   515‚Üí        this.process.kill('SIGKILL');
   516‚Üí        
   517‚Üí        reject(new OptimizerError({
   518‚Üí          type: 'ERROR',
   519‚Üí          request_id: requestId,
   520‚Üí          error_code: 'TIMEOUT',
   521‚Üí          error_message: `No heartbeat received for ${elapsed}ms`,
   522‚Üí          recovery_suggestion: 'Optimizer may be stuck. Consider reducing problem size.'
   523‚Üí        }));
   524‚Üí      }
   525‚Üí    }, this.HEARTBEAT_INTERVAL_MS);
   526‚Üí  }
   527‚Üí  
   528‚Üí  private stopHeartbeatMonitor(): void {
   529‚Üí    if (this.heartbeatTimeout) {
   530‚Üí      clearInterval(this.heartbeatTimeout);
   531‚Üí      this.heartbeatTimeout = null;
   532‚Üí    }
   533‚Üí  }
   534‚Üí}
   535‚Üí
   536‚Üíclass OptimizerError extends Error {
   537‚Üí  readonly errorCode: string;
   538‚Üí  readonly requestId: string;
   539‚Üí  readonly recoverySuggestion: string;
   540‚Üí  readonly stackTrace?: string;
   541‚Üí  
   542‚Üí  constructor(msg: ErrorMessage) {
   543‚Üí    super(msg.error_message);
   544‚Üí    this.name = 'OptimizerError';
   545‚Üí    this.errorCode = msg.error_code;
   546‚Üí    this.requestId = msg.request_id;
   547‚Üí    this.recoverySuggestion = msg.recovery_suggestion;
   548‚Üí    this.stackTrace = msg.stack_trace;
   549‚Üí  }
   550‚Üí}
   551‚Üí```
   552‚Üí
   553‚Üí**C++ Implementation Notes:**
   554‚Üí
   555‚ÜíThe C++ binary must:
   556‚Üí1. Install signal handlers for SIGSEGV, SIGABRT, SIGFPE
   557‚Üí2. Send heartbeat messages every 5 seconds during solve
   558‚Üí3. Catch exceptions and emit ERROR messages before exit
   559‚Üí4. Flush stdout after every message (line-buffered)
   560‚Üí
   561‚Üí```cpp
   562‚Üí// Signal handler example (C++)
   563‚Üívoid signalHandler(int signal) {
   564‚Üí    nlohmann::json error;
   565‚Üí    error["type"] = "ERROR";
   566‚Üí    error["request_id"] = currentRequestId;
   567‚Üí    error["error_code"] = (signal == SIGSEGV) ? "SEGFAULT" : "INTERNAL_ERROR";
   568‚Üí    error["error_message"] = strsignal(signal);
   569‚Üí    error["stack_trace"] = captureStackTrace();
   570‚Üí    error["recovery_suggestion"] = "Internal optimizer error. Check input data.";
   571‚Üí    
   572‚Üí    std::cout << error.dump() << std::endl;
   573‚Üí    std::cout.flush();
   574‚Üí    
   575‚Üí    _exit(128 + signal);
   576‚Üí}
   577‚Üí```
   578‚Üí
   579‚ÜíThe Constraint Aggregation Layer ensures consistency between Strategic and Tactical planning by computing capacity envelopes.
   580‚Üí
   581‚Üí### 2.5.1 Purpose
   582‚Üí
   583‚ÜíThe Strategic Planner operates at quarterly granularity and cannot evaluate detailed daily constraints. However, it must not create plans that the Tactical Scheduler will reject. The Constraint Aggregation Layer bridges this gap.
   584‚Üí
   585‚Üí### 2.5.2 Aggregation Process
   586‚Üí
   587‚Üí```typescript
   588‚Üíinterface ConstraintEnvelope {
   589‚Üí  period: {
   590‚Üí    start: Date;  // Quarter start
   591‚Üí    end: Date;    // Quarter end
   592‚Üí  };
   593‚Üí  
   594‚Üí  // Track capacity (aggregated from daily track availability)
   595‚Üí  track_capacity: {
   596‚Üí    depot_id: string;
   597‚Üí    track_type: 'LIGHT' | 'HEAVY' | 'OVERHAUL';
   598‚Üí    total_slot_days: number;      // Sum of available days in quarter
   599‚Üí    reserved_slot_days: number;   // Already committed
   600‚Üí    available_slot_days: number;  // Remaining capacity
   601‚Üí  }[];
   602‚Üí  
   603‚Üí  // Labor capacity (aggregated from weekly man-hour projections)
   604‚Üí  labor_capacity: {
   605‚Üí    depot_id: string;
   606‚Üí    regular_hours: number;        // Total regular hours in quarter
   607‚Üí    overtime_hours: number;       // Total overtime available
   608‚Üí    committed_hours: number;      // Already scheduled
   609‚Üí    available_hours: number;      // Remaining capacity
   610‚Üí  }[];
   611‚Üí  
   612‚Üí  // Material capacity (aggregated from inventory projections)
   613‚Üí  material_capacity: {
   614‚Üí    depot_id: string;
   615‚Üí    part_category: string;        // e.g., 'BOGIE_KIT', 'BRAKE_SET'
   616‚Üí    expected_available: number;   // Projected inventory
   617‚Üí    committed: number;            // Reserved for scheduled PMs
   618‚Üí    available: number;            // Remaining for new scheduling
   619‚Üí  }[];
   620‚Üí  
   621‚Üí  // Constraints from tactical model
   622‚Üí  blocked_trains: {
   623‚Üí    train_id: string;
   624‚Üí    blocked_weeks: number;        // Weeks unavailable in quarter
   625‚Üí    reason: string;
   626‚Üí  }[];
   627‚Üí}
   628‚Üí
   629‚Üíclass ConstraintAggregationService {
   630‚Üí  /**
   631‚Üí   * Generate constraint envelope for strategic planning
   632‚Üí   */
   633‚Üí  async generateEnvelope(
   634‚Üí    quarterStart: Date,
   635‚Üí    quarterEnd: Date,
   636‚Üí    depotIds: string[]
   637‚Üí  ): Promise<ConstraintEnvelope> {
   638‚Üí    // 1. Aggregate track availability
   639‚Üí    const trackCapacity = await this.aggregateTrackCapacity(
   640‚Üí      quarterStart, quarterEnd, depotIds
   641‚Üí    );
   642‚Üí    
   643‚Üí    // 2. Aggregate labor capacity
   644‚Üí    const laborCapacity = await this.aggregateLaborCapacity(
   645‚Üí      quarterStart, quarterEnd, depotIds
   646‚Üí    );
   647‚Üí    
   648‚Üí    // 3. Aggregate material projections
   649‚Üí    const materialCapacity = await this.aggregateMaterialCapacity(
   650‚Üí      quarterStart, quarterEnd, depotIds
   651‚Üí    );
   652‚Üí    
   653‚Üí    // 4. Identify blocked trains from tactical constraints
   654‚Üí    const blockedTrains = await this.identifyBlockedTrains(
   655‚Üí      quarterStart, quarterEnd
   656‚Üí    );
   657‚Üí    
   658‚Üí    return {
   659‚Üí      period: { start: quarterStart, end: quarterEnd },
   660‚Üí      track_capacity: trackCapacity,
   661‚Üí      labor_capacity: laborCapacity,
   662‚Üí      material_capacity: materialCapacity,
   663‚Üí      blocked_trains: blockedTrains
   664‚Üí    };
   665‚Üí  }
   666‚Üí  
   667‚Üí  /**
   668‚Üí   * Validate strategic allocation against constraint envelope
   669‚Üí   */
   670‚Üí  async validateStrategicAllocation(
   671‚Üí    allocation: StrategicAllocation,
   672‚Üí    envelope: ConstraintEnvelope
   673‚Üí  ): Promise<ValidationResult> {
   674‚Üí    const violations: ConstraintViolation[] = [];
   675‚Üí    
   676‚Üí    // Check track capacity
   677‚Üí    for (const trackReq of allocation.track_requirements) {
   678‚Üí      const available = envelope.track_capacity.find(
   679‚Üí        t => t.depot_id === trackReq.depot_id && 
   680‚Üí             t.track_type === trackReq.track_type
   681‚Üí      );
   682‚Üí      
   683‚Üí      if (available && trackReq.slot_days > available.available_slot_days) {
   684‚Üí        violations.push({
   685‚Üí          type: 'TRACK_OVERCAPACITY',
   686‚Üí          severity: 'HARD',
   687‚Üí          message: `${trackReq.track_type} track at ${trackReq.depot_id} overbooked by ${trackReq.slot_days - available.available_slot_days} days`
   688‚Üí        });
   689‚Üí      }
   690‚Üí    }
   691‚Üí    
   692‚Üí    // Check labor capacity
   693‚Üí    // Check material capacity
   694‚Üí    // ... similar validation logic
   695‚Üí    
   696‚Üí    return {
   697‚Üí      valid: violations.filter(v => v.severity === 'HARD').length === 0,
   698‚Üí      violations
   699‚Üí    };
   700‚Üí  }
   701‚Üí}
   702‚Üí```
   703‚Üí
   704‚Üí---
   705‚Üí
   706‚Üí## 2.6 Key Design Principles
   707‚Üí
   708‚Üí### 2.6.1 Explainability First
   709‚Üí
   710‚ÜíEvery optimization decision must be traceable to specific constraints:
   711‚Üí
   712‚Üí- **Run assignments**: Include explanation factors in API response
   713‚Üí- **PM scheduling**: Log which constraints drove date selection
   714‚Üí- **Man-hour adjustments**: Record rationale for each proposed change
   715‚Üí- **Strategic allocations**: Document constraint envelope used
   716‚Üí
   717‚Üí### 2.6.2 Graceful Degradation
   718‚Üí
   719‚ÜíThe system must provide useful output even when optimal solutions are impossible:
   720‚Üí
   721‚Üí- **C++ solver timeout**: Fall back to TypeScript greedy solver
   722‚Üí- **Material unavailability**: Flag but continue with labor optimization
   723‚Üí- **Constraint conflicts**: Return least-bad solution with violation list
   724‚Üí- **Data quality issues**: Proceed with validated subset, quarantine bad records
   725‚Üí
   726‚Üí### 2.6.3 Audit Everything
   727‚Üí
   728‚ÜíFor 12-year government contract compliance:
   729‚Üí
   730‚Üí- **All schedule changes**: Who, when, what, why
   731‚Üí- **All baseline publications**: Snapshot contents, approval chain
   732‚Üí- **All scenario promotions**: Delta applied, approval
   733‚Üí- **All system decisions**: Optimizer inputs/outputs preserved
   734‚Üí
   735‚Üí### 2.6.4 Stable Long-Term Planning
   736‚Üí
   737‚ÜíPrevent "jittery" forecasts that erode planner trust:
   738‚Üí
   739‚Üí- **Published baselines**: Immutable reference points
   740‚Üí- **Drift thresholds**: Only re-plan when variance exceeds 5%
   741‚Üí- **Dampening**: Strategic forecasts use rolling averages, not daily deltas
   742‚Üí- **Explicit triggers**: Users control when strategic re-planning occurs
   743‚Üí

## 2.2 Component Responsibilities

### 2.2.1 Frontend (Infinite Canvas SPA)

| Responsibility | Implementation |
|----------------|----------------|
| Timeline rendering | deck.gl WebGL layers with semantic zoom |
| Strategic view | Heatmap showing PM density per train per year |
| Tactical view | Gantt blocks for 12-week PM schedule |
| Operational view | Detail cards with technician, track, duration |

### 2.4.4 Error Handling Protocol

**CRITICAL:** The C++ optimizer communicates via stdin/stdout streams. Stream failures (segfaults, hangs) must be detectable by the TypeScript worker.

```typescript
// Worker-side error handling (TypeScript)
class OptimizerWorker {
  private process: ChildProcess;
  private heartbeatTimeout: NodeJS.Timeout | null = null;
  private lastHeartbeat: number = 0;
  
  private readonly HEARTBEAT_INTERVAL_MS = 5000;
  private readonly HEARTBEAT_TIMEOUT_MS = 15000;  // 3 missed heartbeats
  
  async solve(request: OptimizerInput): Promise<OptimizerOutput> {
    return new Promise((resolve, reject) => {
      // Start heartbeat monitor
      this.startHeartbeatMonitor(request.request_id, reject);
      
      // Handle stream events
      this.process.stdout.on('data', (chunk) => {
        for (const line of chunk.toString().split('\n')) {
          if (!line.trim()) continue;
          
          try {
            const msg = JSON.parse(line);
            
            switch (msg.type) {
              case 'HEARTBEAT':
                this.lastHeartbeat = Date.now();
                break;
                
              case 'PROGRESS':
                this.emitProgress(msg);
                break;
                
              case 'RESULT':
                this.stopHeartbeatMonitor();
                resolve(msg);
                break;
                
              case 'ERROR':
                this.stopHeartbeatMonitor();
                reject(new OptimizerError(msg));
                break;
            }
          } catch (parseError) {
            this.logger.warn({ line }, 'Failed to parse optimizer output');
          }
        }
      });
      
      // Handle process crash
      this.process.on('exit', (code, signal) => {
        if (code !== 0) {
          this.stopHeartbeatMonitor();
          reject(new OptimizerError({
            type: 'ERROR',
            request_id: request.request_id,
            error_code: signal === 'SIGSEGV' ? 'SEGFAULT' : 'INTERNAL_ERROR',
            error_message: `Process exited with code ${code}, signal ${signal}`,
            recovery_suggestion: 'Check optimizer logs and input data'
          }));
        }
      });
      
      // Send request
      this.process.stdin.write(JSON.stringify(request) + '\n');
    });
  }
  
  private startHeartbeatMonitor(requestId: string, reject: Function): void {
    this.lastHeartbeat = Date.now();
    
    this.heartbeatTimeout = setInterval(() => {
      const elapsed = Date.now() - this.lastHeartbeat;
      
      if (elapsed > this.HEARTBEAT_TIMEOUT_MS) {
        this.stopHeartbeatMonitor();
        
        // Kill hung process
        this.process.kill('SIGKILL');
        
        reject(new OptimizerError({
          type: 'ERROR',
          request_id: requestId,
          error_code: 'TIMEOUT',
          error_message: `No heartbeat received for ${elapsed}ms`,
          recovery_suggestion: 'Optimizer may be stuck. Consider reducing problem size.'
        }));
      }
    }, this.HEARTBEAT_INTERVAL_MS);
  }
  
  private stopHeartbeatMonitor(): void {
    if (this.heartbeatTimeout) {
      clearInterval(this.heartbeatTimeout);
      this.heartbeatTimeout = null;
    }
  }
}

class OptimizerError extends Error {
  readonly errorCode: string;
  readonly requestId: string;
  readonly recoverySuggestion: string;
  readonly stackTrace?: string;
  
  constructor(msg: ErrorMessage) {
    super(msg.error_message);
    this.name = 'OptimizerError';
    this.errorCode = msg.error_code;
    this.requestId = msg.request_id;
    this.recoverySuggestion = msg.recovery_suggestion;
    this.stackTrace = msg.stack_trace;
  }
}
```

**C++ Implementation Notes:**

The C++ binary must:
1. Install signal handlers for SIGSEGV, SIGABRT, SIGFPE
2. Send heartbeat messages every 5 seconds during solve
3. Catch exceptions and emit ERROR messages before exit
4. Flush stdout after every message (line-buffered)

```cpp
// Signal handler example (C++)
void signalHandler(int signal) {
    nlohmann::json error;
    error["type"] = "ERROR";
    error["request_id"] = currentRequestId;
    error["error_code"] = (signal == SIGSEGV) ? "SEGFAULT" : "INTERNAL_ERROR";
    error["error_message"] = strsignal(signal);
    error["stack_trace"] = captureStackTrace();
    error["recovery_suggestion"] = "Internal optimizer error. Check input data.";
    
    std::cout << error.dump() << std::endl;
    std::cout.flush();
    
    _exit(128 + signal);
}
```

The Constraint Aggregation Layer ensures consistency between Strategic and Tactical planning by computing capacity envelopes.

### 2.5.1 Purpose

The Strategic Planner operates at quarterly granularity and cannot evaluate detailed daily constraints. However, it must not create plans that the Tactical Scheduler will reject. The Constraint Aggregation Layer bridges this gap.

### 2.5.2 Aggregation Process

```typescript
interface ConstraintEnvelope {
  period: {
    start: Date;  // Quarter start
    end: Date;    // Quarter end
  };
  
  // Track capacity (aggregated from daily track availability)
  track_capacity: {
    depot_id: string;
    track_type: 'LIGHT' | 'HEAVY' | 'OVERHAUL';
    total_slot_days: number;      // Sum of available days in quarter
    reserved_slot_days: number;   // Already committed
    available_slot_days: number;  // Remaining capacity
  }[];
  
  // Labor capacity (aggregated from weekly man-hour projections)
  labor_capacity: {
    depot_id: string;
    regular_hours: number;        // Total regular hours in quarter
    overtime_hours: number;       // Total overtime available
    committed_hours: number;      // Already scheduled
    available_hours: number;      // Remaining capacity
  }[];
  
  // Material capacity (aggregated from inventory projections)
  material_capacity: {
    depot_id: string;
    part_category: string;        // e.g., 'BOGIE_KIT', 'BRAKE_SET'
    expected_available: number;   // Projected inventory
    committed: number;            // Reserved for scheduled PMs
    available: number;            // Remaining for new scheduling
  }[];
  
  // Constraints from tactical model
  blocked_trains: {
    train_id: string;
    blocked_weeks: number;        // Weeks unavailable in quarter
    reason: string;
  }[];
}

class ConstraintAggregationService {
  /**
   * Generate constraint envelope for strategic planning
   */
  async generateEnvelope(
    quarterStart: Date,
    quarterEnd: Date,
    depotIds: string[]
  ): Promise<ConstraintEnvelope> {
    // 1. Aggregate track availability
    const trackCapacity = await this.aggregateTrackCapacity(
      quarterStart, quarterEnd, depotIds
    );
    
    // 2. Aggregate labor capacity
    const laborCapacity = await this.aggregateLaborCapacity(
      quarterStart, quarterEnd, depotIds
    );
    
    // 3. Aggregate material projections
    const materialCapacity = await this.aggregateMaterialCapacity(
      quarterStart, quarterEnd, depotIds
    );
    
    // 4. Identify blocked trains from tactical constraints
    const blockedTrains = await this.identifyBlockedTrains(
      quarterStart, quarterEnd
    );
    
    return {
      period: { start: quarterStart, end: quarterEnd },
      track_capacity: trackCapacity,
      labor_capacity: laborCapacity,
      material_capacity: materialCapacity,
      blocked_trains: blockedTrains
    };
  }
  
  /**
   * Validate strategic allocation against constraint envelope
   */
  async validateStrategicAllocation(
    allocation: StrategicAllocation,
    envelope: ConstraintEnvelope
  ): Promise<ValidationResult> {
    const violations: ConstraintViolation[] = [];
    
    // Check track capacity
    for (const trackReq of allocation.track_requirements) {
      const available = envelope.track_capacity.find(
        t => t.depot_id === trackReq.depot_id && 
             t.track_type === trackReq.track_type
      );
      
      if (available && trackReq.slot_days > available.available_slot_days) {
        violations.push({
          type: 'TRACK_OVERCAPACITY',
          severity: 'HARD',
          message: `${trackReq.track_type} track at ${trackReq.depot_id} overbooked by ${trackReq.slot_days - available.available_slot_days} days`
        });
      }
    }
    
    // Check labor capacity
    // Check material capacity
    // ... similar validation logic
    
    return {
      valid: violations.filter(v => v.severity === 'HARD').length === 0,
      violations
    };
  }
}
```

## 2.6 Key Design Principles

### 2.6.1 Explainability First

Every optimization decision must be traceable to specific constraints:

- **Run assignments**: Include explanation factors in API response
- **PM scheduling**: Log which constraints drove date selection
- **Man-hour adjustments**: Record rationale for each proposed change
- **Strategic allocations**: Document constraint envelope used

### 2.6.2 Graceful Degradation

The system must provide useful output even when optimal solutions are impossible:

- **C++ solver timeout**: Fall back to TypeScript greedy solver
- **Material unavailability**: Flag but continue with labor optimization
- **Constraint conflicts**: Return least-bad solution with violation list
- **Data quality issues**: Proceed with validated subset, quarantine bad records

### 2.6.3 Audit Everything

For 12-year government contract compliance:

- **All schedule changes**: Who, when, what, why
- **All baseline publications**: Snapshot contents, approval chain
- **All scenario promotions**: Delta applied, approval
- **All system decisions**: Optimizer inputs/outputs preserved

### 2.6.4 Stable Long-Term Planning

Prevent "jittery" forecasts that erode planner trust:

- **Published baselines**: Immutable reference points
- **Drift thresholds**: Only re-plan when variance exceeds 5%
- **Dampening**: Strategic forecasts use rolling averages, not daily deltas
- **Explicit triggers**: Users control when strategic re-planning occurs
